rationale: All four words are present with correct text, weights, and colors: SHIP/FAST/BREAK in heavy white caps and NOTHING larger in the specified yellow, centered as a block with consistent line spacing and nothing clipped. Timing deviates from the brief: the first ~20% of the strip is empty black and words are still punching in around the 25-30% mark, so the stagger reads slower than ~0.25s apart, and the hold then occupies most of the remaining tiles. The exit is never visible in any tile — the block is fully intact in the last strip tile and simply gone at the 100% still, so the alternating horizontal fly-out cannot be confirmed and reads as an abrupt cut; the pulse on NOTHING is imperceptible across tiles, making the long hold feel frozen. Typography itself is clean and high-contrast, but the dead air at the start and unverified exit choreography mean it needs a timing revision pass before shipping.
